name: bog-witch-hut
summary: A lone hut on stilts (or a stump, or a slumped mound) at the edge of a bog. Smoke from a crooked chimney, bone charms on the door, a cauldron outside. More outdoor than indoor — the swamp is part of the home.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: irregular — hut footprint plus surrounding bog yard

accent.purpose: hut floorboards — a small raised interior

primary_palette.note: the hut itself as centerpiece — cauldron outside the door
outdoor workspace — the witch works in front of the hut
stilted hut over open bogwater — walkway of planks to dry ground

story_prompts: the witch is home — tea is ready, she was expecting someone (maybe you)
the witch is out — familiars guard the hut, cauldron still bubbling
the witch is dead — the charms on the door have gone silent, something moved in
not a witch — a healer hiding from a town that would burn her
a hag wears the witch's face — the real witch is bones under the floorboards

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: dim bog-dusk — low ambient, greenish tint, firelight from cauldron and hut windows

anti_patterns: the hut is not a building-room — it is a prop/structure in a wilderness space
do not clean up the yard — bones, jars, hung herbs should crowd the workspace
the bog is part of the room — wet ground, reeds, dead trees on the perimeter
cauldron sits outside, not inside — witch work is weather work
